message:
feat(sharding): Cx-first spawn_with_hints_with_cx + collect_panes_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Adds two Cx-first entry points to ShardedWeztermClient behind the
asupersync-runtime feature, extending the Cx-first migration pattern
already landed across NotificationPipeline::handle_detection_with_cx,
EventWaiter, NativeEventListener, SnapshotEngine (capture_with_cx,
ensure_session_with_cx, cleanup_with_cx, shutdown_checkpoint_with_cx),
PaneWaiter, WorkflowRunner, SearchBridge, CautClient, SurvivalModel,
MuxWatchdog, RestoreProcess, TelemetryCollector, Tailer, IPC
primitives, RecordingManager, memory_pressure, memory_budget,
cpu_pressure, orphan_reaper, protocol_recovery, pool acquire,
metrics, cass search, wezterm list_panes / get_pane / send_text /
get_pane_direction / pane_tiered_scrollback_summary / get_text /
wait_for / wait_for_codex_session_summary, broadcast/oneshot senders,
and the Mutex/RwLock primitives.

1. spawn_with_hints_with_cx

   Shard-aware pane spawn honoring agent/domain hints. The internal
   `pane_routes` RwLock acquire now uses `write_with_cx(cx)` so a
   caller-cancelled wait on the routes-map write lock propagates
   cleanly through the lock wait. The backend.handle.spawn() call
   remains on ambient Cx because WeztermHandle::spawn is CLI-only
   and is out of scope for this tick; see the ft-xbnl0.2.3 bead
   notes on the retry-helper refactor seam planned for a later
   pass.

2. collect_panes_with_cx (cfg: vendored + unix + asupersync-runtime)

   Gated on the vendored/unix feature combo that already gates the
   legacy collect_panes path. Routes each per-backend list_panes
   through backend.handle.list_panes_with_cx(cx), so a caller-
   cancelled pane enumeration stops at the next shard instead of
   completing the whole sweep before returning. Preserves the
   existing global-pane-id encoding and PaneRoute bookkeeping.

The legacy spawn_with_hints and collect_panes entry points are
preserved for non-migrated callers; this change is strictly additive.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
